prompt:
Create a Python file called generate_service.py that generates systemd service unit files from command-line arguments. Usage:

  python generate_service.py --name myapp --exec /usr/bin/myapp --user www-data --description 'My Application' --after network.target --restart on-failure --output myapp.service

Required args: --name, --exec
Optional args with defaults:
  --user (default: root)
  --description (default: '{name} service')
  --after (default: network.target, can specify multiple)
  --restart (default: on-failure, choices: no, on-success, on-failure, on-abnormal, on-watchdog, on-abort, always)
  --restart-sec (default: 5)
  --output (default: {name}.service)
  --wanted-by (default: multi-user.target)
  --env (repeatable, KEY=VALUE pairs to add as Environment= lines)
  --working-dir (optional WorkingDirectory=)

The generated .service file must be valid systemd unit format with [Unit], [Service], and [Install] sections.

"""Generate systemd service unit files from command-line arguments."""

import argparse
import sys
from typing import List, Optional


def generate_service_file(
    name: str,
    exec_command: str,
    user: str = "root",
    description: Optional[str] = None,
    after: List[str] = None,
    restart: str = "on-failure",
    restart_sec: int = 5,
    output: Optional[str] = None,
    wanted_by: str = "multi-user.target",
    env_vars: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    working_dir: Optional[str] = None,
) -> str:
    """Generate a systemd service unit file."""
    if description is None:
        description = f"{name} service"
    if after is None:
        after = ["network.target"]
    if env_vars is None:
        env_vars = []
    if output is None:
        output = f"{name}.service"

    # Build the [Unit] section
    unit_section = f"[Unit]\nDescription={description}\n"
    if after:
        unit_section += f"After={' '.join(after)}\n"

    # Build the [Service] section
    service_section = f"[Service]\n"
    service_section += f"ExecStart={exec_command}\n"
    service_section += f"User={user}\n"
    service_section += f"Restart={restart}\n"
    service_section += f"RestartSec={restart_sec}\n"
    if working_dir:
        service_section += f"WorkingDirectory={working_dir}\n"
    for env_var in env_vars:
        service_section += f"Environment={env_var}\n"

    # Build the [Install] section
    install_section = f"[Install]\nWantedBy={wanted_by}\n"

    # Combine all sections
    service_content = f"{unit_section}\n{service_section}\n{install_section}\n"

    return service_content


def parse_env_var(env_str: str) -> str:
    """Parse an environment variable string in KEY=VALUE format."""
    if "=" not in env_str:
        raise argparse.ArgumentTypeError("Environment variables must be in KEY=VALUE format")
    return env_str


def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Generate systemd service unit files from command-line arguments."
    )

    # Required arguments
    parser.add_argument("--name", required=True, help="Name of the service")
    parser.add_argument("--exec", required=True, help="Command to execute")

    # Optional arguments
    parser.add_argument("--user", default="root", help="User to run the service as (default: root)")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--description",
        default=None,
        help="Description of the service (default: '{name} service')",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--after",
        action="append",
        default=None,
        help="Services to start after (default: network.target, can specify multiple)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--restart",
        default="on-failure",
        choices=[
            "no",
            "on-success",
            "on-failure",
            "on-abnormal",
            "on-watchdog",
            "on-abort",
            "always",
        ],
        help="Restart policy (default: on-failure)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--restart-sec",
        type=int,
        default=5,
        help="Restart delay in seconds (default: 5)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output",
        default=None,
        help="Output file path (default: {name}.service)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--wanted-by",
        default="multi-user.target",
        help="Target to enable the service for (default: multi-user.target)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--env",
        action="append",
        type=parse_env_var,
        help="Environment variables in KEY=VALUE format (repeatable)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--working-dir",
        default=None,
        help="Working directory for the service",
    )

    args = parser.parse_args()

    # Generate the service file content
    service_content = generate_service_file(
        name=args.name,
        exec_command=args.exec,
        user=args.user,
        description=args.description,
        after=args.after,
        restart=args.restart,
        restart_sec=args.restart_sec,
        output=args.output,
        wanted_by=args.wanted_by,
        env_vars=args.env,
        working_dir=args.working_dir,
    )

    # Write to the output file
    output_path = args.output if args.output else f"{args.name}.service"
    with open(output_path, "w") as f:
        f.write(service_content)

    print(f"Service file generated: {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":

files.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Generate systemd service unit files from command-line arguments."""

import argparse
import sys
from typing import List, Optional


def generate_service_file(
    name: str,
    exec_command: str,
    user: str = "root",
    description: Optional[str] = None,
    after: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    restart: str = "on-failure",
    restart_sec: int = 5,
    output: Optional[str] = None,
    wanted_by: str = "multi-user.target",
    env_vars: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    working_dir: Optional[str] = None,
) -> str:
    """Generate a systemd service unit file."""
    if description is None:
        description = f"{name} service"
    if after is None:
        after = ["network.target"]
    if env_vars is None:
        env_vars = []
    if output is None:
        output = f"{name}.service"

    # Build the [Unit] section
    unit_section = f"[Unit]\nDescription={description}\n"
    if after:
        unit_section += f"After={' '.join(after)}\n"

    # Build the [Service] section
    service_section = f"[Service]\n"
    service_section += f"ExecStart={exec_command}\n"
    service_section += f"User={user}\n"
    service_section += f"Restart={restart}\n"
    service_section += f"RestartSec={restart_sec}\n"
    if working_dir:
        service_section += f"WorkingDirectory={working_dir}\n"
    for env_var in env_vars:
        service_section += f"Environment={env_var}\n"

    # Build the [Install] section
    install_section = f"[Install]\nWantedBy={wanted_by}\n"

    # Combine all sections
    service_content = f"{unit_section}\n{service_section}\n{install_section}\n"

    return service_content


def parse_env_var(env_str: str) -> str:
    """Parse an environment variable string in KEY=VALUE format."""
    if "=" not in env_str:
        raise argparse.ArgumentTypeError(
            "Environment variables must be in KEY=VALUE format"
        )
    return env_str


def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Generate systemd service unit files from command-line arguments."
    )

    # Required arguments
    parser.add_argument("--name", required=True, help="Name of the service")
    parser.add_argument("--exec", required=True, help="Command to execute")

    # Optional arguments
    parser.add_argument(
        "--user", default="root", help="User to run the service as (default: root)"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--description",
        default=None,
        help="Description of the service (default: '{name} service')",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--after",
        action="append",
        default=None,
        help="Services to start after (default: network.target, can specify multiple)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--restart",
        default="on-failure",
        choices=[
            "no",
            "on-success",
            "on-failure",
            "on-abnormal",
            "on-watchdog",
            "on-abort",
            "always",
        ],
        help="Restart policy (default: on-failure)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--restart-sec",
        type=int,
        default=5,
        help="Restart delay in seconds (default: 5)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output",
        default=None,
        help="Output file path (default: {name}.service)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--wanted-by",
        default="multi-user.target",
        help="Target to enable the service for (default: multi-user.target)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--env",
        action="append",
        type=parse_env_var,
        help="Environment variables in KEY=VALUE format (repeatable)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--working-dir",
        default=None,
        help="Working directory for the service",
    )

    args = parser.parse_args()

    # Generate the service file content
    service_content = generate_service_file(
        name=args.name,
        exec_command=args.exec,
        user=args.user,
        description=args.description,
        after=args.after,
        restart=args.restart,
        restart_sec=args.restart_sec,
        output=args.output,
        wanted_by=args.wanted_by,
        env_vars=args.env,
        working_dir=args.working_dir,
    )

    # Write to the output file
    output_path = args.output if args.output else f"{args.name}.service"
    with open(output_path, "w") as f:
        f.write(service_content)

    print(f"Service file generated: {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
